creator: jseo@netsol.com
text:
> -----Original Message-----
 > From:	Seo, June 
 > Sent:	mm/15/99 11:05
 > To:	'Dag-Erling Smorgrav'
 > Subject:	RE: i386/12102: can't boot using the installation boot
 > diskette made by RAWRITE
 > 
 > Thank you very much for your response!!!
 > 
 > I tried with two brand new floppies and got the same error. I followed
 > instructions on "The Complete FreeBSD" by  Greg Lehey to make the boot
 > floppy from the CD-ROM as follows:
 > 
 > E:\> tools\rawrite floppies\boot.flp
 > 
 > I found different instructions to make a boot floppy in FreeBSD.org site.
 > I made two floppies following the instructions:
 > 
 > E:\> tools\fdimage floppies\kern.flp A:
 > E:\> tools\fdimage floppies\mfsroot.flp A:
 > 
 > I was able to boot into the installation kernel using the two floppies. I
 > am new to FreeBSD. So I am not sure why two sources of information are
 > different and what they mean. Anyway, I am so glad that I can boot. 
 > 
 > Thank you again for your help!
 > June Seo
 > 
 > 
 > -----Original Message-----
 > From:	Dag-Erling Smorgrav [SMTP:des@flood.ping.uio.no]
 > Sent:	mm/13/99 10:52
 > To:	jseo@netsol.com
 > Cc:	freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.ORG
 > Subject:	Re: i386/12102: can't boot using the installation boot
 > diskette made by RAWRITE
 > 
 > jseo@netsol.com writes:
 > > /kernel text=0x18be72 data=0x2eb35c+0xldcc0 zf_read: fill error
 > > 
 > > elf_loadexec: archsw.readin.failed
 > > Can't load 'kernel'
 > 
 > Sounds like a bad floppy. Did you try a different floppy?
 > 
 > DES
 > -- 
 > Dag-Erling Smorgrav - des@flood.ping.uio.no
